acceptance_criteria: notebook contains no generic unit visual builder call
final_sanity records core residuals below tolerance
the applied lab classifies both direct and opposite examples
chapter_goal: Turn Euclidean distance preservation into affine orthogonal data and inspect translations, reflections, rotations, orientation, and composition.
computational_checks: orthogonal residuals for every matrix used
pairwise distance preservation on sample constellations
artifact existence and nonzero file size
concept_inventory: reflections in hyperplanes and their fixed sets
rotation in R2 and rotation axis in R3
composition law for affine orthogonal maps
orientation behavior through signed area and cross product

library_routing_table.why: durable static diagram and exact numeric distance matrix

library_routing_table.representation: mirror line, normal direction, fixed points, perpendicular connectors
library_routing_table.why: the construction is planar and metric
rotating the scene makes axis and orientation more inspectable than a flat projection
exact signs and trigonometric identities should not depend on floating-point samples
readable ledger of determinant, fixed-set, and residual data

proof_visualization_strategy: Use finite constellations, fixed-set diagrams, determinant ledgers, and exact symbolic identities as proof scaffolds for the appendix propositions.

source_span_read.book: Andrew Pressley, Elementary Differential Geometry, Second Edition
source_span_read.source_use: orientation only; no prose, figures, exercises, screenshots, or page crops copied
source_span_read.title: Isometries of Euclidean spaces

visual_sequence.inspection_target: unchanged distance matrix under a direct affine isometry
mirror fixed set and perpendicular point pairs
rotated orthonormal frame and signed area preservation
two reflections compose to a rotation and signs multiply
visual_sequence.validation: matrix residual and determinant product table

visual_sequence.inspection_target: axis rotation versus plane reflection in R3
